codeReview.summary: The canonical-event location fix and [0,0] guards are well targeted, and the accompanying regression tests directly cover the precision-selection logic. I did not find a correctness issue in the implementation itself that should block the milestone.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The frontend-worker skill should explicitly document fallback validation strategies for MapLibre/Three.js surfaces in headless Chrome instead of only treating WebGL interaction failures as a reason to return to orchestrator.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-28T00-17-00-320Z__cross-area-map-data-flow__3001e869-1cae-4fc9-972f-5a4cee0ee0b9.json records expected headless WebGL failures and verifies the feature through indirect flows; `.factory/library/user-testing.md` previously covered the 3D globe limitation but not MapLibre GL or canvas-based visualizations.

summary: Pass. Event coordinates now prefer the best-located article and the map surfaces guard against invalid [0,0] markers.
